title: Search Relevance and Discovery
summary: Looked at how users find existing content through search. Exact-keyword expectations clashed with how items were named, so relevant results were missed when wording differed. Users rarely tried a second query; they assumed the content did not exist.

quotes.text: I searched once, didn't see it, and assumed we just didn't have it. Turns out it was there under a different name.
quotes.speaker: Participant 12, power user
